verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And since the three first years after St. Chrysostom was ordained Priest, A.D. 386–8, seem completely filled up, and the Homilies in St. Matthew were probably prior to these, it is most likely that they were not begun before A.D. 390, while those on some of the Epistles of St. Paul seem to have come after them, and still before the year 398, in which he was removed to Constantinople.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In either city there were numerous heretics of the sect against which he is most careful to supply arguments, the Anomœans, who held that the Son is not even of like [much less of the same] substance with the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And even in his less generally controversial works, we often meet with discussions of their tenets.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But in these Homilies he is continually meeting with texts which they perverted to the maintenance of their heresy, and turning them into weapons for its confutation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this he usually does with great success, since the Catholic doctrine of the true and perfect Godhead, united in One Person with true and perfect Manhood, affords a key that easily opens texts which most stubbornly resist any confused notion of an inferior Divinity, or an unreal Humanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The texts urged by the heretic, put to this test, are found not really to belong to him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They are not even arguments so far for his view of the case, but perfectly consistent with the truth always held by the Church.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There may remain a few cases, after attentive study, in which it is difficult to be sure what is the exact meaning, or even whether a given text speaks of the Godhead or of the Manhood, but as to the general doctrine of the whole Scripture, or the consistency of that doctrine with any and every text therein contained, there is no reasonable doubt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There are those whose faith seems to tremble on the balance when such a passage of Scripture is under discussion, but this must be either from an inveterate habit of doubting, or an imperfect apprehension of the real meaning of the Catholic doctrine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The most skillful commentator may occasionally fall into a critical error, but no one who has ever fully entered into the sense of Holy Scripture will dream of the alternative being between such and such an exposition and the acceptance of heresy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Enough is clear to make us very sure what will be the doctrine of any difficult passage, though we may be in doubt of its interpretation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: St. Chrysostom is usually right, and not only so, but most ingenious in detecting the rhetorical connection of sentiments and arguments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If anywhere he fails, it is from some over-refinement in rhetorical analysis, and not from any want of apprehension of the main truths concerned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In the first volume of the Benedictine edition there is a series of Homilies against the Anomœans, in the first of which he states that he had been unwilling for some time to enter on the controversy, for fear of driving away hearers who held those opinions, but that he had now taken it up at their earnest request.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These Homilies were delivered some time before those on St. John, beginning in the first year after his ordination with those “On the Incomprehensible Nature of God,” in opposition to the pretensions of that sect to the perfect knowledge of Divine things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the Benedictine Editor refers to them as containing a more complete array of the positive evidence of St. John to the Catholic doctrines than even this commentary affords.
